chapter_title.en: Chapter on a Man Who is Killed and Found in Dismembered Parts

text: 5377 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنِ اَلْفَضْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ فَيُوجَدُ رَأْسُهُ فِي قَبِيلَةٍ وَ وَسَطُهُ وَ صَدْرُهُ وَ يَدَاهُ فِي قَبِيلَةٍ وَ اَلْبَاقِي فِي قَبِيلَةٍ قَالَ "دِيَتُهُ عَلَى مَنْ وُجِدَ فِي قَبِيلَتِهِ صَدْرُهُ وَ يَدَاهُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَيْهِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5377 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Al-Fadl ibn Uthman, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who was killed, and his head was found in one tribe, his torso and arms in another tribe, and the rest of his body in yet another tribe.
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon the tribe where his torso and arms were found, and the funeral prayer is to be performed over him."
